free_text: Independent production cross sections for Hg isotopes
from the reaction of 600-MeV 18-O with Pb
An intermediate energy heavy-ion
accelerator
In Chinese.
The two papers contain more detailed information about
experimental device.
Decay data.
The average beam was 2.2 particle nA.
An on-line gas thermochromatographic separation
device combined with a running-rabbit system
Veto signals from beta+ decays.
Three different irradiation-detection periods
were employed: first, the cycle was a 120-S
irradiation followed a 420-S g-ray detection, and a
total of 143 samples were measured;
second, 10-min irradiation followed by 60-min detection
and three samples;
third, 100-min irradiation followed by 48-H detection
for one sample.
A molten natural lead target with a thickness of about
5.2 g/cm2 (thick enough to stop the 600-MeV 18O
incident beam). The temperature of the molten lead
target material was continuously kept at about 700.C
during the 18-O beam irradiation.
A large bismuth germanate detector (14x7x5 cm3)
and a beta-ray detector (unknown type)
Measurement errors of the experimental cross
sections are estimated to be 15% to 30%, including the
systematic error from collection efficiency
determination, statistical error of gamma-ray counts,
and count contamination to the characteristic gamma-
ray used in cross-section calculation from neighboring
gamma rays. A probable additional error would come
from the uncertainty of the data for gamma-ray
branching ratio.
A probable example is the unusual lower measurement
cross section for 193-Hg, which has not been clarified
clear so far.
-By authors. To evaluate independent cross sections
for Hg isotopes, special care has been exercised over
the following difficulties:
1. Half-lives of some Hg isotopes are very short in
comparison with the time elapsed before starting the
measurement (typically 20 sec after irradiation);
2. For some Hg isotopes gamma-decay schemes and/or
absolute gamma-ray branching ratios have not been
measured;
3. Gamma rays which should be characteristic of some
Hg isotopes including relevant daughter or
granddaughter nuclides are too weak to be identified
or overlapping with other gamma rays.
Tbl.I
By F.Ch
Last checking has been done.

(half-life=46sec). It is unknown, what decay was used
